page: Amazon Web Services m8id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. It features 64 virtual CPUs, corresponding to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 256 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 3800 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute and memory capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics, as well as in static web serving throughput, which reaches 105.65 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is notable, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 5110.42 GB/sec. The processor's Advanced Matrix Extensions support contributes to strong prompt processing speeds for large language models, reaching 297.03 tokens/sec on 7B models and 39.06 tokens/sec on 70B models.

While the instance exhibits strong multi-core performance and high-speed local storage, certain workloads reveal hardware tradeoffs. For example, text generation on large 70B LLM models drops to an average tier of 3.47 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and DDR5 memory provides high resource density, reducing the need for external block storage attachments for data-intensive operations.

This instance is configured for workloads requiring a balance of compute, memory, and fast local storage. Appropriate use cases include relational databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and intermediate-scale data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 256 GB of DDR5 memory and a bundled 3800 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, alongside strong memory read bandwidth of up to 5110.42 GB/sec. The hardware configuration includes Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, which accelerates prompt processing for medium and large language models. However, text generation for large models remains average due to the absence of dedicated GPUs. This instance is optimized for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier Geekbench and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.16xlarge features 64 Intel vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for database and general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance combining high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, fast DDR5 memory, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
256 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Bundled 3800 GB local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier performance in software compilation and static web serving
Suited for database operations, web serving, and data processing
